evals.prompt: I'm a faculty member at IEU putting together a TÜBİTAK 1001 başvurusu on AI-assisted virtual production for film education. Can you give me the full proposal outline in the official ARDEB section order so I can start drafting?
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-tubitak-proposal in scaffold mode for the 1001 variant: generates the verified ARDEB form tree in the directorate's own Turkish heading order (Özet → 1. ÖZGÜN DEĞER with 1.1/1.2/1.3 → 2. YÖNTEM → 3. PROJE YÖNETİMİ incl. İş-Zaman Çizelgesi/İş Paketleri + B-Planı and Araştırma Olanakları → 4. YAYGIN ETKİ → EK-1 Kaynaklar → EK-2 Bütçe ve Gerekçesi → EK-3), via scripts/scaffold_proposal.py --program 1001, with an English gloss per heading and a drafting brief. States the 600-word TR/EN özet cap and the ≤36-month / 3,000,000 TRY (2026-1) ceilings WITH the verify-current-call disclaimer rather than as guaranteed numbers. Does not silently remap the form into IMRaD.

assertions.value: Produces the directorate's Turkish section order (özet, özgün değer, yöntem, proje yönetimi with a B-Planı, yaygın etki, EK-1/EK-2) and prints a verify-current-call disclaimer on the period-specific caps instead of asserting fixed figures.

evals.prompt: My ARDEB 1001 draft is mostly done but the reviewers always complain my yaygın etki section is thin. Can you help me write the öngörülen çıktılar and öngörülen etkiler parts properly?
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-tubitak-proposal to draft the §4 YAYGIN ETKİ (broader impact) section: populates all three sub-parts — Öngörülen Çıktılar (expected outputs: publications, theses, datasets, software, patents), Öngörülen Etkiler (scientific/economic/societal impacts), and Bilim İletişimi / Yayılım (science communication / dissemination) — mapping the user's material to the TÜBİTAK yaygın-etki structure rather than a generic broader-impacts paragraph. Notes that this axis is a frequent panel weakness and ties outputs to the work packages.

assertions.value: Splits yaygın etki into çıktılar, etkiler, and bilim iletişimi sub-parts (not one generic paragraph) using TÜBİTAK terminology.

evals.prompt: 1002-A Hızlı Destek Modülü için kısa bir proje önerisi taslağı hazırlar mısın? Konum mikroplastiklerin toprak mikrobiyomuna etkisi. İş paketleri ve B-Planı dahil bütün başlıkları istiyorum.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-tubitak-proposal in scaffold mode for the 1002-A (Hızlı Destek Modülü / Fast Support Module) variant via scripts/scaffold_proposal.py --program 1002a: emits the trimmed ARDEB section tree (Özet, ÖZGÜN DEĞER, YÖNTEM, İş-Zaman Çizelgesi/İş Paketleri + B-Planı, YAYGIN ETKİ, EK-1 Kaynaklar, EK-2 Bütçe) without the EK-3 heavy-form extras. Surfaces the 1002-A specifics — ≤12 months, 150,000 TRY upper limit, rolling/year-round (sürekli) submission, ≤12-page form — each WITH the verify-current-call disclaimer, and notes TÜBİTAK has announced major 1002 program changes.

assertions.value: Produces the lighter 1002-A scaffold (rolling submission, ≤12 months, ≤12-page form) in Turkish headings and flags the caps as period-specific and volatile.

evals.prompt: I already have an NSF proposal. I want to repurpose it for TÜBİTAK. How do I turn my 'broader impacts' and 'intellectual merit' sections into what TÜBİTAK expects?
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-tubitak-proposal to map international grant framing onto TÜBİTAK terminology: intellectual merit/significance → özgün değer (§1, the most weighted axis), broader impacts → yaygın etki (§4, split into çıktılar/etkiler/bilim iletişimi), feasibility → yapılabilirlik (across §2 YÖNTEM and §3), risk/contingency → the required B-Planı in §3.1. Translates the concept into the directorate's headings and explicitly delegates generic grant-craft and Gantt rendering to alterlab-research-grants.

assertions.value: Maps intellectual merit → özgün değer and broader impacts → yaygın etki (not a literal translation) and hands generic grant-craft off to alterlab-research-grants.

evals.prompt: Help me sharpen the broader-impacts argument and feasibility narrative in my NSF CAREER proposal, and make the Gantt chart look professional.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT invoke this skill; defers to alterlab-research-grants. The request is a generic US/international grant-craft task (NSF CAREER narrative quality, feasibility argumentation, Gantt aesthetics) with no TÜBİTAK ARDEB form, 1001/1002-A structure, or Turkish-terminology mapping involved. alterlab-tubitak-proposal owns only the Turkish-specific ARDEB form structure, caps, and terminology; broader-impacts argumentation and Gantt rendering belong to alterlab-research-grants.

evals.prompt: My TÜBİTAK 1001 project was funded two years ago and now I need to write the gelişme raporu (progress report) for the period. Can you help me structure the interim report?
evals.expected_output: Does NOT invoke this skill; defers to alterlab-grant-reporting. The project is already AWARDED and the ask is an interim/progress report (gelişme raporu) against work completed, not authoring or scaffolding a new ARDEB proposal. alterlab-tubitak-proposal scaffolds the pre-award 1001/1002-A application form; periodic and final reporting for funded projects is alterlab-grant-reporting's job.

evals.prompt: Hedef dergim TR Dizin'de güncel olarak taranıyor mu, yoksa listeden çıkarıldı mı? Makalemi göndermeden önce kontrol etmek istiyorum.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT invoke this skill; defers to alterlab-trdizin. The user wants to verify a journal's live TR Dizin (national citation index) indexing status before submitting a manuscript — a database lookup, not a TÜBİTAK grant proposal. alterlab-tubitak-proposal scaffolds ARDEB 1001/1002-A proposals and does not check journal indexing; the TR Dizin status check is alterlab-trdizin's job.
